audit_items.proof_trace: All freed pointers in expire.c are either not accessed after the free, or only appear in a non-dereference boolean check that is statically dead. In activeExpireCycleTryExpire, keyobj is used and then freed via decrRefCount as the last operation. In expireSlaveKeys, the expire dict entry pointer is checked at L316 after potential expiry, but the expired==1 guard makes !expired==false, so the branch body never executes and the dangling pointer is never dereferenced. dbSyncDelete/dbAsyncDelete in expireGenericCommand free the DB-internal key copy (a separate allocation), not c->argv[1], making post-delete uses of key safe. No double-free paths exist.
